steps.title: Identify what the blank needs
steps.content: The blank begins the second sentence and modifies the plural noun “letters”. The word must be a plural determiner that agrees in number with “letters” and points back to the eighteen letters just mentioned.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, keep the right one
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'One': Violates determiner-noun agreement — “one” is a singular determiner and cannot modify the plural noun “letters.” ✗
  • Choice B — 'That': Violates determiner-noun agreement — “that” is a singular demonstrative determiner and does not agree with the plural noun “letters.” ✗
  • Choice C — 'This': Violates determiner-noun agreement — “this” is a singular demonstrative determiner and does not agree with the plural noun “letters.” ✗
  • Choice D — 'These': “These” is the plural demonstrative determiner, correctly agreeing in number with the plural noun “letters.” ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
